mission.title: traefik: v2: support `NOT` in `rules` definition
mission.description: v2: support `NOT` in `rules` definition. Requested by 84+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature

### What did you expect to see?

I would like to see support for `NOT` (or `!`) in addition to `AND` and `OR` for defining routing `rules`, just to enable support for proper

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "v2: support `NOT` in `rules` definition" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/5228

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
